content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
George F. Carpinello 
Union University 
Albany Law School 
80 New Scotland Avenue 
Albany, NY 
12208 
Dear Mr. Carpinello: 
october 15, 1987 
Re: 
87-256 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on October 14, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact John McLean, an attorney in the 
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly yours, 
. 
' 
~JJu1-~~ 11, 1 . 0;t.»fld-i~ 
Diane M. 
Griff~ths 
~A R~_ 
General Counsel 
U 
DMG:plh 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


GEORGE F. CARPINELLO 
ASSOCIATE PROFESSOR OF LAW 
UNION UNIVERSITY 
ALBANY l.A W SCHOOl. 
80 NEW SCOTLAND AVENUE 
ALBANY. NEW YORK 12208 
518·445·2311 
October 6, 1987 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Sir or Madam: 
We are conducting research on state ethics laws or 
regulations relating to conflicts of interest for state 
legislators. 
We are especially interested in ethics rules 
relating to legislators who are attorneys engaging in private 
practice of law. 
We would appreciate it if you could send us copies of any 
regulations or advisory opinions by your commission or any other 
applicable commission concerning lawyer/legialator conflicts of 
interests. 
Also, we would be interested in receiving copies of 
any new statutes relating to government ethics passed within the 
past two years. 
If there is any cost associated with receiving these 
materials, please advise us by telephone or mail and we will 
forward to you the necessary fee. 
Thank you for your help and cooperation. 
SinC1f~ 
George F. Carpinello 
GFC:ep

sections.question: We are conducting research on state ethics laws or regulations relating to conflicts of interest for state legislators. We are especially interested in ethics rules relating to legislators who are attorneys engaging in private practice of law. We would appreciate it if you could send us copies of any regulations or advisory opinions by your commission or any other applicable commission concerning lawyer/legislator conflicts of interests. Also, we would be interested in receiving copies of any new statutes relating to government ethics passed within the past two years.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester is seeking copies of California regulations, advisory opinions, and recent statutes concerning conflicts of interest rules for state legislators, particularly those who are attorneys in private practice.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated October 6, 1987) and the FPPC's acknowledgment/receipt letter (dated October 15, 1987). The FPPC letter is procedural in nature, not a substantive advice response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present in the substantive correspondence.

embedding.summary: This document contains an incoming request from Professor George F. Carpinello at Albany Law School requesting copies of California ethics regulations and advisory opinions regarding conflicts of interest for legislator-attorneys, along with recent government ethics statutes. The FPPC's response letter acknowledges receipt of the request and sets expectations for processing time.
